topic: A neighbor wants to install a fence
raw_output:
[story 1]

The sunlight seemed too bright today, a harsh glare reflecting off the freshly painted wood of Mrs. Henderson’s new fence. I watched from behind my lace curtains, my shoulders sinking further into the worn upholstery of the armchair. Every muscle in my body felt heavy, like wet sand. When Mr. Henderson knocked on my door, his voice was cheerful, a bright, insistent sound that made my chest tighten. “Hello there! Just wanted to chat about the boundary line.” I managed a weak smile, the effort costing me more energy than I possessed. I just nodded, my gaze fixed on a dust mote dancing in a sunbeam. I longed for the silence of my own room, a quiet space where the world didn't demand so much of me.

[story 2]

I stood by the window, tracing patterns on the condensation gathering on the glass. The sheer volume of the neighbor's enthusiasm for the new barrier felt like a physical weight pressing down on my chest. Each word he spoke about stakes and posts seemed to vibrate through the floorboards and settle deep in my bones, leaving me feeling utterly depleted. When he finally finished his explanation, I could only manage a small, almost inaudible murmur. My eyes kept drifting toward the floor, unable to meet his gaze. I felt a profound lack of motivation, a deep inertia that made even lifting my arm to wave feel like scaling a mountain.

[story 3]

The sound of the hammer striking the post was sharp and insistent, cutting through the afternoon quiet. I was sitting on the porch swing, my feet dangling just above the lawn, but I didn't move. My head felt fuzzy, as if the world was moving in slow motion, and the effort required to process the activity before me was monumental. When the neighbor leaned over the fence, gesturing emphatically with his hands, I simply stared past him, my breathing shallow. A dull ache settled behind my eyes, a heavy blanket that made engaging with the day feel like an unnecessary exertion. I wished the ground would simply swallow me whole.
